_meta.descripcion: Extensión del banco diagnóstico al nivel Advanced (A1 Foundation + A2 Structures). Mismo esquema que periplo_diagnostico_basic.json. Se añade a BANK.topics y se extiende PLACE_MAP.
_meta.integracion: Añade estos 8 temas al final de BANK.topics (después de possessive_pronouns). El orden del array sigue siendo el gradiente de dificultad: Basic (8) -> Advanced (8). Extiende PLACE_MAP para los nuevos índices.
_meta.nota_skills: evaluaciones.html solo trae sub-skills oficiales para Basic B1. TODOS los temas de Advanced traen skills PROPUESTAS por pedagogía (basadas en errores típicos de hispanohablantes). Revísalas y súbelas a evaluaciones.html cuando quieras.
_meta.nota_ladder: Según la escalera adaptativa: Advanced arranca con pasados (past_simple, luego past_continuous como contraste). Si el alumno domina la 'A Mix', sube a Pro. Trueno de banda = fin del examen.

_legend.tool: cuál de los 18 componentes renderiza la pregunta
_legend.skill: punto de fallo que mide (tag para el rubro)
_legend.auto: true=se autocalifica (Examen 1) | false=la revisa el maestro (Examen 2)
_legend.L1: true=caza un error típico de transferencia del español
_legend.contrast: true=las opciones malas vienen de tiempos/estructuras que compiten

topics.vocab: Irregular/Regular verbs + Nouns Pack 1 Adv (Success & Failure) + Adj Pack 1 Adv

questions.q: Last year she ___ a major milestone.
"Did he achieved his goal?" is correct.

questions.audioText: He finally reached his goal after years of setbacks.
questions.q: When did he reach his goal?
Sort each verb by how it forms the past tense.
After many obstacles, the underdog ___ (finish) the race.
She ___ hard for her breakthrough. (study)

questions.answer: When did she start her comeback?

context: A: Did he reach his goal?
options: Yes, he did. He worked hard.
Yes, he reached. He work hard.
Yes, he was. He worked hard.
questions.answer: Yes, he did. He worked hard.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
Did she achieved her ambition?
He no finished the project on time.
questions.answer: He didn't finish the project on time.

questions.q: Why she failed the first time?
questions.answer: Why did she fail the first time?

topics.vocab: Irregular verbs + Nouns Pack 2 Adv (Truth & Lies) + Adj Pack 2 Adv

questions.q: If I knew the truth, I ___ you.
In the second conditional, "If I were you" uses 'were' for all persons.

questions.audioText: If she told the truth, everyone would trust her.
questions.q: Is this a real or an imaginary situation?
Match each 'if' clause with its result.
If he ___ (know) about the betrayal, he would be furious.
questions.hint: pasado simple en la cláusula if

questions.answer: If I were you, I would confess.

questions.q: If they trusted us, we ___ (share) everything.

context: A: What would you do if you found a cover-up?

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: If I had money, I would travel.
If I would have money, I would travel.
If she were honest, I'd trust her.
If she would be honest, I'd trust her.
answers: If I had money, I would travel.
If she were honest, I'd trust her.

questions.q: If I would know the truth, I would tell you.
questions.answer: If I knew the truth, I would tell you.

questions.q: If I was a whistleblower, I would expose them.
questions.answer: If I were a whistleblower, I would expose them.

questions.q: If they trusted me, I would told them everything.
questions.answer: If they trusted me, I would tell them everything.

topics.vocab: Verbs Pack 3 + Nouns Pack 3 Adv (Media & Entertainment) + Adj Pack 3 Adv

questions.q: At 9 PM, millions ___ watching the premiere.
Past continuous describes an action in progress in the past.

questions.audioText: The audience was leaving when the celebrity arrived.
questions.q: What was the audience doing when the celebrity arrived?
Order the sentences into a logical mini-story.
tokens: It was a quiet night at the studio.
The crew was setting up the cameras.
Suddenly, the lead actor walked in late.
questions.answer: It was a quiet night at the studio. The crew was setting up the cameras. Suddenly, the lead actor walked in late. Everyone stopped and stared.

questions.q: While the critic ___ (write) the review, the news broke.

questions.answer: What were you watching when it happened?

questions.q: The influencer was ___ live. (stream)

context: A: What were you doing when the sequel was released?

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: I was watching TV when she called.
I watching TV when she called.
While they were filming, it started to rain.
While they filming, it started to rain.
answers: I was watching TV when she called.
While they were filming, it started to rain.

questions.q: The fans waiting outside all night.
questions.answer: The fans were waiting outside all night.

questions.q: The critics was writing their reviews.
questions.answer: The critics were writing their reviews.

questions.q: She was writeing a review of the blockbuster.
questions.answer: She was writing a review of the blockbuster.

topics.grammar: Was/Were Going To (past plans not fulfilled)
topics.vocab: Verbs Pack 4 + Nouns Pack 4 Adv (Regrets & Turning Points) + Adj Pack 4 Adv

questions.q: I ___ going to take that opportunity, but I hesitated.
"I was going to call, but I didn't" describes a plan that did NOT happen.

questions.audioText: She was going to apologize, but she changed her mind.
questions.q: Did she apologize?
Match each subject with was/were going to.
We were going to ___ (make) a decision, but we reached a crossroads.

questions.answer: What were you going to do?

questions.q: He ___ going to take the risk after all.

context: A: Weren't you going to move abroad?
options: I was, but I had second thoughts.
I was going move, but no.
I were going to, but no.
questions.answer: I was, but I had second thoughts.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
I was going to called you last night.
questions.answer: I was going to call you last night.

questions.q: We going to take that path, but we changed our minds.
questions.answer: We were going to take that path, but we changed our minds.

questions.q: They was going to reconcile, but it was too late.
questions.answer: They were going to reconcile, but it was too late.

topics.vocab: Verbs Pack 6 + Nouns Pack 5 Adv (Attraction & Identity) + Adj Pack 5 Adv

questions.q: I ___ fall for the wrong type of person.
"I used to smoke" means it was a past habit that has stopped.

questions.audioText: She used to be very guarded, but now she opens up easily.
questions.q: How is she now?
Which sentence is INCORRECT?
He ___ (have) a different type, but his preferences changed.

questions.answer: Did you use to trust people?

questions.q: I ___ use to believe in chemistry.

context: A: Did you use to be different?
options: Yes, I used to be more spontaneous.
Yes, I use to be more spontaneous.
Yes, I used to being spontaneous.
Yes, I was used to be spontaneous.
questions.answer: Yes, I used to be more spontaneous.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: Did you use to live here?
Did you used to live here?
answers: Did you use to live here?

questions.q: I didn't used to like him.
questions.answer: I didn't use to like him.

questions.q: She used to falling for unavailable people.
questions.answer: She used to fall for unavailable people.

questions.q: Did he used to be so charming?
questions.answer: Did he use to be so charming?

topics.vocab: Nouns Pack 6 Adv (Ambition & Competition) + Adj Pack 6 Adv

questions.q: This contender is ___ than the others.
She is ___ competitor in the league.

questions.audioText: Their strategy was better than ours, but ours was the cheapest.
questions.q: Whose strategy was cheaper?
Match each adjective with its comparative form.
This year's benchmark is ___ (high) than last year's.
It was the ___ negotiation of her career. (tough)

questions.answer: This deal is better than the last one.

context: A: Who is the best contender?
options: She is the most strategic of all.
She is most strategic of all.
She is more strategic that all.
questions.answer: She is the most strategic of all.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: He is more ambitious than me.
He is more ambitiouser than me.
This is the most hardest pursuit.
answers: He is more ambitious than me.

questions.q: This promotion is more important that the last one.
questions.answer: This promotion is more important than the last one.

questions.q: She is more smarter than her competitor.
questions.answer: She is smarter than her competitor.

questions.q: He is the most strongest contender in the room.
questions.answer: He is the strongest contender in the room.

topics.vocab: Nouns Pack 7 Adv (Mystery & Crime) + Adj Pack 7 Adv

questions.q: The suspect ___ disappeared is still missing.
The motive is unclear. The killer ___ be someone close.

questions.audioText: The witness who saw everything could be lying.
questions.q: What is true about the witness?
Match each noun type with its relative pronoun.
The evidence ___ they found changed the verdict.

questions.answer: The accomplice could still be nearby.

questions.q: The detective ___ theory was correct solved the case.

options: The man who left the clue.
The man which left the clue.
The man who he left the clue.
The man whose left the clue.
questions.answer: The man who left the clue.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: The witness who he testified lied.
He must to be the culprit.

questions.q: The man who he committed the crime escaped.
questions.answer: The man who committed the crime escaped.

questions.q: The suspect could to be innocent.
The detective which solved the case retired.
questions.answer: The detective who solved the case retired.

topics.grammar: Present Perfect Continuous (+ still / yet / already / not anymore)
topics.vocab: Nouns Pack 8 Adv (Systems & Society) + Adj Pack 8 Adv

questions.q: The institution ___ been reforming its policies.
They have been protesting ___ 2020.

questions.audioText: Activists have been fighting inequality for decades.
questions.q: How long have they been fighting?
Sort each time expression into FOR or SINCE.
I've ___ finished the report, so I'm free now.
The government ___ been ignoring the reform.

questions.answer: How long have you been working here?

questions.q: She has been studying governance ___ five years.

context: A: What have you been up to?
options: I've been working on a project.
I been working on a project.
I've been work on a project.
questions.answer: I've been working on a project.

questions.q: I haven't seen the final results ___.
Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: He has been studying for hours.
He has been study for hours.
They have been waiting since noon.
They has been waiting since noon.
answers: He has been studying for hours.
They have been waiting since noon.

questions.q: I have been living here since five years.
questions.answer: I have been living here for five years.

questions.q: She has been work all day.
questions.answer: She has been working all day.

questions.q: The authorities has been ignoring the problem.
questions.answer: The authorities have been ignoring the problem.

questions.q: I haven't done it already.

discrimination_set._nota: Discriminación de TIEMPOS de Advanced. Prueba si el alumno distingue cuál tiempo usar (past simple vs continuous, present perfect vs past simple, etc.). Sugerencia: checkpoint al entrar a Advanced en la escalera adaptativa.

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence.
options: I was watch TV when she called.
I was watching TV when she called.
I watched TV when she was call.
I watching TV when she called.
questions.answer: I was watching TV when she called.

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence (with 'yesterday').
Choose the correct sentence for a past habit that is no longer true.
options: I used to live in Paris.
I use to live in Paris.
I was living in Paris always.
I am used to live in Paris.
questions.answer: I used to live in Paris.

questions.q: Choose the correct imaginary (unreal) sentence.
options: If I have time, I help you.
If I had time, I would help you.
If I will have time, I would help.
If I would have time, I help.
questions.answer: If I had time, I would help you.

questions.q: I was going to called you, but I forgot.
questions.answer: I was going to call you, but I forgot.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: I have been waiting for an hour.
I have waiting for an hour.
I have been finished my work.
answers: I have been waiting for an hour.

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence with 'since 2010'.
She is more taller than her competitor.
questions.answer: She is taller than her competitor.

exam2_productive._nota: Producción para el Examen 2 (auto:false, lo revisa el maestro). Temas de Advanced.

questions.prompt: Tell a short story (4-5 sentences) about a time something went wrong. Use the past simple and the past continuous.
Talk about how you used to be a few years ago vs. how you are now (3 sentences with 'used to').

questions.q: Si yo fuera rico, viajaría por todo el mundo.
questions.answer: If I were rich, I would travel all over the world.

questions.prompt: What have you been working on lately? Write 3 sentences using the present perfect continuous.

questions.q: Esta ciudad es más cara que la mía, pero es la más segura.
questions.answer: This city is more expensive than mine, but it is the safest.

questions.prompt: Describe a person who changed your life, using a relative clause (who/that/whose).
